title: Wire TopologyAwareFailureModes 'Simulate This' button to handleSimulate
description: At TopologyAwareFailureModes.tsx:508-510, the 'Simulate This' button handler only does console.log. Wire it to the existing handleSimulate callback passing the selected failure mode ID, or remove the button until the feature is implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Simulate This' triggers the cascade animation for the selected failure mode
No console.log remains in the handler

title: Show feedback when Submit CMD is dropped due to no leader in Raft
description: At raft.ts:355-356, submitCommand silently returns void when no leader exists. At DistributedModule.tsx:3674, the UI calls it without checking the result. Users click 'Submit CMD' and see nothing happen. Fix: return a boolean from submitCommand; show a toast/badge in the UI when false.
acceptanceCriteria: submitCommand returns false when no leader exists
UI shows visible feedback (toast, red flash, or disabled button) when command is dropped
Existing tests continue to pass

title: Split DistributedModule.tsx (4904 lines) into ~15 focused files
description: DistributedModule.tsx is a 4904-line god file containing 11 canvas components, 3 panel components, and a hook with 45 state variables. Extract: (1) hooks/useDistributedModule.ts, (2) canvas/RaftCanvas.tsx through canvas/PaxosCanvas.tsx (11 files), (3) DistributedSidebar.tsx, (4) DistributedProperties.tsx, (5) DistributedBottomPanel.tsx. Keep the barrel export in DistributedModule.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: No file exceeds 500 lines
All 11 simulations still work identically
Each canvas component is independently importable
The hook is testable in isolation

title: Replace inline canvas components with orphaned accessible visualizers
description: Three standalone visualizers exist at components/visualization/distributed/ (RaftVisualizer 391 lines, ConsistentHashRingVisualizer 244 lines, VectorClockDiagram 252 lines) with proper ARIA roles, labels, and framer-motion animations. The main module uses inferior inline equivalents with zero accessibility. Either: (A) replace RaftCanvas/ConsistentHashCanvas/VectorClockCanvas with the standalone versions, adapting props as needed, or (B) port all ARIA attributes from standalone to inline. Option A preferred.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG elements have role='img' with descriptive aria-label
Individual nodes have aria-label attributes
Event log has role='log' with aria-label
Screen reader can identify all interactive elements
At least 3 canvases use the accessible pattern

title: Replace 60+ hardcoded hex colors with design tokens/CSS variables
description: DistributedModule.tsx contains 10+ color maps (ROLE_COLORS, NODE_COLORS, MSG_COLORS, PROCESS_COLORS, REPLICA_COLORS, PHASE_COLORS, etc.) with 60+ hardcoded hex values at lines 132-147, 152-157, 561-564, 1011-1012, 1701-1706, 1910-1915, 2109-2115, 2418, 2658-2664. Plus inline SVG attributes throughout. The Learn panel and standalone visualizers correctly use CSS variables (var(--primary), etc.) and IBM_COLORBLIND from @/lib/visualization/colors. Migrate all hex values to the shared token system.
acceptanceCriteria: Zero hardcoded hex values remain in any canvas component
All colors reference CSS custom properties or the shared color palette
Theme switching (if supported) correctly changes canvas colors
Visual appearance matches current design

title: Add prefers-reduced-motion support to all canvas animations
description: Zero canvas components check prefers-reduced-motion (confirmed by grep). The Raft RAF animation loop at DistributedModule.tsx:244-276, CRDT merge SVG animate elements at 1123-1188, gossip exchange arrows, and all other animations run unconditionally. Add a useReducedMotion() hook that: (1) disables the Raft RAF animation loop, (2) reduces CRDT merge animation to instant state change, (3) removes gossip arrow animations, (4) skips any transition durations above 100ms.
acceptanceCriteria: With prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, all animations complete instantly or are hidden
Raft simulation still functions correctly without animation
No motion-sensitive content displayed without user consent

title: Delete or integrate orphaned standalone visualizers (887 lines dead code)
description: RaftVisualizer.tsx (391 lines), ConsistentHashRingVisualizer.tsx (244 lines), VectorClockDiagram.tsx (252 lines) in components/visualization/distributed/ are never imported. If DIS-024 integrates them, this task becomes 'delete the inline versions'. If DIS-024 ports ARIA to inline versions, this task becomes 'delete the standalone versions'. Either way, 887 lines of dead code must be resolved.
acceptanceCriteria: No unused component files remain in components/visualization/distributed/
No broken imports after deletion

title: Cap event log array at 500 entries to prevent memory leak
description: At DistributedModule.tsx:3628-3634 (and 8+ other locations), setEvents appends without limit: setEvents((prev) => [...prev, ...mapped]). Raft auto-play at 100ms generates ~10 events/second. After 10 minutes: ~6000 events. The bottom panel caps display at 100 (line 3461) but the state array grows unboundedly. Fix: use prev.slice(-(500 - mapped.length)) before spreading.
acceptanceCriteria: Event array never exceeds 500 entries
Most recent events are preserved (ring buffer behavior)
UI event log continues to display correctly
No visible behavior change for sessions under 5 minutes

title: Write dedicated test suites for 4 untested engines
description: These engines have zero dedicated tests: (1) Lamport timestamps — test receive rule max(local, received)+1, edge case local > received. (2) Saga pattern — test compensation reverse order, fail at step 1 boundary. (3) MapReduce — test empty input, single word, special character stripping. (4) CRDT — test OR-Set concurrent add/remove, LWW tie-breaking by replica ID, PNCounter negative values, GCounter non-negative constraint. Additionally, gossip.test.ts (28 lines), cap-theorem.test.ts (23 lines), two-phase-commit.test.ts (24 lines), paxos.test.ts (25 lines) are minimal and should be expanded.
acceptanceCriteria: lamport-timestamps.test.ts exists with >= 8 test cases
saga.test.ts exists with >= 6 test cases
map-reduce.test.ts exists with >= 6 test cases
crdt.test.ts exists with >= 12 test cases (3 per CRDT type)
All tests pass

title: Add 'WHY' explanations to step descriptions in all engines
description: All 11 engines produce step descriptions that say WHAT happened but never WHY. For example, raft.ts:427-432 says 'starting election for term 1' but not 'because no heartbeat received within timeout'. map-reduce.ts:95 says 'emit 3 pairs' but not 'each word becomes a (word,1) pair for aggregation'. Add an optional 'insight' or 'reason' field to event types and populate it with beginner-friendly WHY explanations. Display in a tooltip or dedicated panel section.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 event types per engine include a 'reason' or 'insight' field
Insights use plain English, no jargon without definition
UI displays insights (tooltip, expandable section, or side panel)
A beginner can understand WHY each step occurred

title: Add contextual empty state guidance per simulation
description: Currently, 2PC/Saga/Paxos show bare 'Run the simulation to see X' messages. Raft shows a static diagram with no hint of interactivity. Add per-simulation empty states with: (1) a brief 'what you'll learn' sentence, (2) a pulsing or highlighted call-to-action pointing to the first button to click, (3) an optional illustration or simplified preview animation.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of the 11 simulations has a unique empty state message
At least the primary action button is visually highlighted on first load
A beginner knows what to click within 3 seconds of seeing the module

title: Fix SVG font sizes below 11px minimum (25+ instances)
description: Throughout DistributedModule.tsx, SVG text elements use fontSize='8', '9', '10' — at least 25 instances. At lines 360, 378, 770, 787, 932 and throughout all canvas components. Set minimum fontSize='11' for all SVG text. For truly small labels (e.g., term badges), consider using a tooltip on hover instead.
acceptanceCriteria: No SVG text element has fontSize below 11
All labels remain readable at default zoom
Layout doesn't overflow due to larger text

title: Increase sidebar touch targets to 44px minimum for mobile
description: Sidebar simulation buttons at DistributedModule.tsx:2952-2966 use py-2.5 px-3 (~36px height), under the 44x44px WCAG touch target minimum. Increase to py-3 for 44px.
acceptanceCriteria: All sidebar buttons meet 44px minimum height
Control panel buttons also meet 44px minimum
Touch interaction works on mobile without mis-taps

title: Extract duplicate SeededRandom class to shared utility
description: Identical xorshift32 SeededRandom class exists in both raft.ts:118-139 and gossip.ts:43-64. Extract to src/lib/utils/seeded-random.ts and import from both locations.
acceptanceCriteria: Single SeededRandom class in src/lib/utils/
Both raft.ts and gossip.ts import from shared location
All existing tests pass unchanged

title: Add end-of-simulation feedback for step-based sims
description: At DistributedModule.tsx:4038,4088,4138,4226 — step-based sims (2PC, Saga, MapReduce, Paxos) silently clamp at last step via Math.min. User clicks Step repeatedly with no indication they've reached the end. Add visible 'Simulation complete' badge and/or disable the Step button.
acceptanceCriteria: Visible indication when simulation reaches final step
Step button is disabled or shows 'Complete' state
Auto-play stops naturally at end with clear feedback

title: Lazy-instantiate engine classes on first sim selection
description: At DistributedModule.tsx:3513-3607, all 7 stateful engine classes are instantiated at mount (RaftCluster, ConsistentHashRing, VectorClockSimulation, GossipProtocol, CRDTSimulation, CAPCluster, LamportSimulation). Wrap each in a lazy ref pattern that instantiates on first access.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine classes are not instantiated until their sim is first selected
Switching to a new sim for the first time creates the engine without delay
Memory usage at mount is reduced

title: Remove console.log from TopologyAwareFailureModes production code
description: At TopologyAwareFailureModes.tsx:509, a console.log statement is left in the click handler. Remove it.
acceptanceCriteria: No console.log in TopologyAwareFailureModes.tsx
ESLint no-console rule passes

title: Reorder sidebar simulations for optimal learning path
description: Current order is ad-hoc. Optimal learning order based on prerequisite dependencies: (1) CAP Theorem (framework), (2) Lamport Timestamps (simple ordering), (3) Vector Clocks (extends Lamport), (4) Consistent Hashing, (5) Gossip Protocol, (6) CRDTs, (7) Two-Phase Commit, (8) Saga Pattern, (9) Raft, (10) Paxos, (11) MapReduce. Reorder the SIMULATIONS array at DistributedModule.tsx:72-128 and add difficulty tags.
acceptanceCriteria: SIMULATIONS array is reordered by learning prerequisite order
Each simulation entry has a 'difficulty' field (beginner/intermediate/advanced)
Sidebar displays difficulty indicator (color dot or tag)

title: Add Linearizability & Consistency Models interactive simulation
description: Critical curriculum gap: No coverage of consistency models (Linearizability, Sequential, Causal, Eventual). This is in MIT 6.5840 LEC 8, DDIA Ch 9, and top-5 interview topics. Build an interactive simulation that shows the same set of read/write operations producing different outcomes under each consistency model. Users toggle between models and see how the same operations resolve differently.
acceptanceCriteria: New engine in src/lib/distributed/consistency-models.ts
Supports at least 4 models: Linearizable, Sequential, Causal, Eventual
Visual shows 3 nodes with read/write operations and different outcomes per model
Properties panel explains each model with real-world database examples

title: Add Replication simulation (Leader-Follower, Multi-Leader, Leaderless/Quorum)
description: Critical curriculum gap: No simulation for replication strategies despite having excellent written content in concepts-data.ts (slug: 'replication'). MIT 6.5840 core topic, DDIA Ch 5 entire chapter, universal interview topic. Build: (1) Leader-Follower with sync/async toggle showing replication lag, (2) Multi-Leader showing write conflicts, (3) Leaderless with adjustable NWR quorum sliders. SEO content already covers the theory — this adds the interactive experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Engine in src/lib/distributed/replication.ts supports 3 modes
Quorum mode lets user adjust N, W, R with sliders and see success/failure
Multi-leader mode shows conflict detection and resolution
Leader-follower shows replication lag visual (async) vs blocking (sync)
Properties panel links to existing SEO content for replication
